title: quercus × rosacea subsp. secalliana
intro: Quercus × rosacea subsp. secalliana is a hybrid of Quercus suber and Quercus faginea, commonly known as the Cork Oak and Portuguese Oak, which produces edible acorns. The medicinal properties of Quercus × rosacea subsp. secalliana lie in its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to act as an antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agent, and it has been used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Quercus × rosacea subsp. secalliana can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its acorns can be dried and ground into a flour for use in baking and cooking.
